subject: docs(conventions): codify useCallback/useMemo omit-by-default rule + track deferred verification items
body:
Closes out the tier-3 wrap-up items that had been flagged but not applied.

- CLAUDE.md AI Notes: new bullet captures the useCallback/useMemo rule
  this codebase follows. Default is OMIT — the audit already stripped 26
  overhead wrappers. Codifying prevents future audits from rediscovering
  and future contributors from reintroducing. Calls out the three
  empirically-verified KEEP categories (data-layer subscription effects,
  BurgerMenu.close into useEscapeKey, page-level clearToast into
  Toast.onDone).
- CLAUDE.md architecture: useAuth moved out of data-hooks listing
  (it's a thin useContext wrapper, not a data layer) and labeled
  "context consumers" alongside the other hook categories.
- TODO.md: adds "Testing Infrastructure" (RTL + jsdom setup so common
  components like Modal / ConfirmDialog / Toast / HeadingInput get
  regression coverage) and "Manual Verification" (browser smoke-test
  of this branch before merge — the three reduction commits passed
  tsc/lint/test but weren't loaded visually).

Also verified KEEPs on all 10 hooks — 5 data-layer hooks explicitly
list their fetchX in subscription effect deps; usePWAInstall /
usePWAUpdate kept under public-API-stability convention; useAuth is
the thin context wrapper with no useCallback in the first place.

No source changes. Verified: `tsc -b` clean, `npm run lint` clean,
`npm test` → 22 passing.

Tags: docs, conventions, todo
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: custom-code-reduction
Semver: patch
